title: Post-flood room triage — full solution
summary: Sort rooms by safety before cleanup begins. This full solution explains decisions, materials, steps, mistakes, failure signs, alternatives, and recovery limits for real offline use.
content:
Sort rooms by safety before cleanup begins. This full solution explains decisions, materials, steps, mistakes, failure signs, alternatives, and recovery limits for real offline use.

Purpose: Post-flood room triage — full solution keeps people away from moving water, electrical hazards, unstable ground, hidden contamination, and rushed cleanup.

Use it when the situation is uncertain, people are tired, and a short checklist is not enough. The method is built around visible notes, reversible steps, official guidance, and protection of the weakest person first.

Working sequence: begin with safety scan; create one written version; assign roles; act in short cycles; check failure signs; document changes; and hand over clearly. This prevents sort rooms by safety before cleanup begins. from becoming a hidden, verbal, or heroic action that nobody else can repeat.

Stop point: stop immediately if the action increases fire, flood, structural, medical, contamination, violence, or evacuation risk.
materials: Visible paper plan titled “Post-flood room triage — full solution” with date, time, location, and owner
Dedicated pencil, tape, marker, and plastic sleeve for sort rooms by safety before cleanup begins. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Small container for critical documents, medication notes, keys, cash, and contact cards Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Phone or radio power plan with one reserved battery source
Clean water, hand hygiene item, light source, and safe footwear near the action point Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Stop/leave sign that can be understood by tired people and visitors
steps: Open with a two-minute safety scan: fire, smoke, water, wires, unstable objects, violence, breathing problems, and missing people connected to post-flood room triage — full solution. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.

Write the exact problem, location, time, people involved, and immediate need before anyone leaves the area for sort rooms by safety before cleanup begins.. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Do not enter moving water, flooded basements, submerged roads, or rooms with electrical uncertainty. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Move valuables upward only if the route stays dry, lit, structurally safe, and reversible. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Mark wet, contaminated, sharp, slippery, and unstable areas separately after the water drops. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Dry, ventilate, photograph, and discard unsafe materials according to local guidance instead of saving everything. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.

Assign one review time for post-flood room triage — full solution and replace the plan if conditions change, official instructions arrive, or the weakest person becomes less safe. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
End with a handover note: what was done, what failed, what remains unsafe, who is responsible, and when the next check must happen. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
warnings: Do not use post-flood room triage — full solution to delay evacuation, emergency calls, medical care, or official orders. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Do not enter fire, floodwater, smoke, damaged structures, chemical contamination, or violent situations to save supplies. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Do not depend on memory alone; tired groups forget times, names, doses, keys, and promises. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Stop sort rooms by safety before cleanup begins. if it creates new danger for children, older adults, disabled people, animals, or rescuers. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
When facts conflict, prefer verified official instructions over social media, rumors, or a single witness. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
common_mistakes: Making post-flood room triage — full solution too complicated for a phone battery, dark room, or exhausted person. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Letting the strongest or loudest person control the plan without checking vulnerable people. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Mixing confirmed facts, guesses, and fears on the same note.
Starting cleanup, travel, or distribution before recording hazards and stop points. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Forgetting to update post-flood room triage — full solution after weather, power, water, health, or official guidance changes. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
alternatives: If paper is missing, use cardboard, a clean wall section, a phone note, or repeated radio wording for sort rooms by safety before cleanup begins.. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
If one person cannot lead, split the role into safety, information, supplies, and welfare checks. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
If the normal route or tool fails, pause and choose a slower option that preserves exits and communication. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
If communication fails, use pre-agreed check-in times and physical signs rather than constant searching. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
failure_signs: People cannot explain the current post-flood room triage — full solution plan in one sentence. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
The plan helps supplies but leaves a person, animal, or exit unprotected. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Different people are following different versions of the instructions.
A hazard is growing faster than the group can control it.
The action depends on light, fuel, battery, medicine, or strength that is almost gone. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue when official evacuation, shelter-in-place, medical, utility, or fire-service instructions say otherwise. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Do not use this plan as a substitute for trained rescue, electrical work, gas work, structural inspection, or medical treatment. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
Do not attempt sort rooms by safety before cleanup begins. alone if the space, people, or animals cannot be observed safely. Context: Post-flood room triage — full solution.
